articles.title: US home prices hit an all-time high as sales slow and mortgage rates rise
articles.summary: Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes slowed in June, but a key measure of home prices climbed to an all-time high, adding to prospective homebuyers’ affordability challenges
articles.content:
US home prices hit an all-time high as sales slow and mortgage rates rise

Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes slowed in June, but a key measure of home prices climbed to an all-time high, adding to prospective homebuyers’ affordability challenges

Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes slowed in June, but a key measure of home prices climbed to an all-time high, adding to prospective homebuyers' affordability challenges.

Existing home sales fell 2.4% last month from May to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.09 million units, the National Association of Realtors said Thursday. Sales rose 2.8% compared with June last year.

The latest sales tally fell short of the roughly 4.21 million pace economists were expecting, according to FactSet.

Home sales have been mostly hovering close to a 4-million annual pace going back to 2023, far short of the historic norm that is closer to 5.2-million.

Sales have remained sluggish this year as mortgage rates have mostly trended higher in the months since the spring as the war between the U.S. and Iran began raising expectations of higher inflation. Still, mortgage rates remain below where they were a year ago.

Home prices continued to rise nationally last month. The U.S. median sales price increased 1.8% in June from a year earlier to $440,600, an all-time high, NAR said. Home prices have risen on an annual basis for 36 months in a row.

articles.title: ConnectOne Bancorp Announces Second Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Date and Conference Call
articles.summary: ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq: CNOB) is scheduled to release its second-quarter 2026 financial results on July 23, 2026, before market open. Following the release, Chairman and CEO Frank Sorrentino III and CFO William S. Burns will host a conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET to...
articles.content:
ConnectOne Bancorp plans to release Q2 2026 financial results on July 23, with a conference call at 10 a.m. ET.

Quiver AI Summary

ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc. has announced that it will release its second-quarter financial results for the period ending June 30, 2026, on July 23, 2026, before the market opens. Following this, the company's management, including Chairman and CEO Frank Sorrentino III and CFO William S. Burns, will host a conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET to discuss the financial performance and operating results. Interested parties can join the call by dialing 1 (585) 542-9983 and are encouraged to register five minutes early. An audio webcast of the call will be accessible to the public via the company's website and will be archived for later access. ConnectOne Bancorp operates ConnectOne Bank and its fintech subsidiary, BoeFly, Inc., providing services aimed at small to middle-market businesses. The company's stock is traded on the Nasdaq under the symbol "CNOB."

Potential Positives

  • ConnectOne Bancorp is scheduled to announce its second quarter financial results on July 23, 2026, indicating a commitment to transparency and shareholder communication.
  • The upcoming conference call will feature key executives, allowing for direct engagement with investors and stakeholders, which can strengthen investor relations.
  • An audio webcast of the conference call will be available to the public, enhancing accessibility to information regarding the company's financial performance.
  • ConnectOne Bancorp's operations in both traditional banking and fintech signify a diversified business model, positioning the company well in the evolving financial services landscape.

Potential Negatives

  • Uncertainty surrounding financial performance results prior to release could lead to speculation and volatility in stock performance.

FAQ

When will ConnectOne Bancorp release its Q2 2026 results?

ConnectOne Bancorp will release its second quarter results on July 23, 2026, before the market opens.

What time is the conference call for Q2 results?

The conference call to discuss the Q2 results is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. ET on July 23, 2026.

Who will host the Q2 2026 earnings call?

The earnings call will be hosted by Chairman Frank Sorrentino III and CFO William S. Burns.

How can I listen to the conference call?

You can listen to the call via the "Investor Relations" link on ConnectOne's website or by calling 1 (585) 542-9983.

Will there be a replay of the conference call available?

Yes, an online archive of the webcast will be available after the call on ConnectOne's website.

ENGLEWOOD CLIFFS, N.J., July 09, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq: CNOB) (the “Company” or “ConnectOne”), parent company of ConnectOne Bank (the “Bank”), today announced that it plans to release results for the second quarter ended June 30, 2026, before the market opens on Thursday, July 23, 2026. Management will also host a conference call and audio webcast at 10:00 a.m. ET on July 23, 2026, to review the Company's financial performance and operating results.

Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Frank Sorrentino III and Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer William S. Burns will host the call. The conference call dial-in number is 1 (585) 542-9983, meeting ID: 646 211 267. Please dial in at least five minutes before the start of the call to register. An audio webcast of the conference call will be available to the public, on a listen-only basis, via the "Investor Relations" link on the Company's website https://www.ConnectOneBank.com or at http://ir.connectonebank.com .

An online archive of the webcast will be available following the completion of the conference call at https://www.ConnectOneBank.com or at http://ir.connectonebank.com .

About ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc.
ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc., is a modern financial services company that operates, through its subsidiary, ConnectOne Bank, and the Bank’s fintech subsidiary, BoeFly, Inc. ConnectOne Bank is a high-performing commercial bank offering a full suite of banking & lending products and services that focus on small to middle-market businesses. BoeFly, Inc. is a fintech marketplace that connects borrowers in the franchise space with funding solutions through a network of partner banks. ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc. is traded on the Nasdaq Global Market under the trading symbol "CNOB," and information about ConnectOne may be found at https://www.connectonebank.com .

articles.title: Ask HN: Any good replacements for "Refined Hacker News?"
articles.summary: Yesterday my browser updated and turned off "Refined Hacker News." It was a very minimalist plugin that made some minor UI changes to Hacker News that I miss. (It turned the upper-right username into a drop-down, and allowed in-line replies to comments.) Are there any replacem...
articles.content:
Yesterday my browser updated and turned off "Refined Hacker News." It was a very minimalist plugin that made some minor UI changes to Hacker News that I miss. (It turned the upper-right username into a drop-down, and allowed in-line replies to comments.)

Are there any replacements for that plugin? I tried a different plugin that has similar features (won't name), but it makes too many cosmetic changes, and, IMO, leaves Hacker News looking too ugly.

articles.title: Khamenei's body arrives in Mashhad for burial after week of processions
articles.summary: Khamenei's body arrives in Mashhad for burial after week of processions While US air strikes hit railways to Mashhad, Iran claims the eight-hour delay to the burial was due to unexpected crowds Mourners gather by Imam Reza shrine for the burial of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, 9 Jul...
articles.content:
Khamenei's body arrives in Mashhad for burial after week of processions

After a week-long mass funeral, the body of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has arrived in the northeastern city of Mashhad, where a burial ceremony will take place as the final step.

The plane carrying Khamenei's body, along with those of his four family members killed alongside him, was escorted by Iranian military jets and arrived at Mashhad's Shahid Hasheminejad Airport early on Thursday.

But Iranian media announced that the burial ceremony will now take place at 2pm local time, eight hours later than originally planned.

The delay came just hours after the US and Iran resumed air strikes following a short-term truce.

While US strikes on railway bridges disrupted travel between Tehran and Mashhad, Iranian officials insist the delay is not linked to the attacks. Instead, Iranian state media claims the postponement is due to an overwhelming crowd of mourners.

Mashhad, the slain leader’s home town, is currently hosting immense crowds from across Iran and Iraq.

The city holds particular religious significance because it is home to the shrine of Imam Reza, a prominent figure for Shia Muslims.

Mohammad Mohammadi Golpayegani, the head of the late leader’s office, stated that Khamenei had explicitly requested to be buried in the city.

Khamenei was assassinated on 28 February in a joint US-Israeli military strike.

Among the massive crowd in Mashhad, mourners held placards reading “Kill Trump” and chanted slogans such as “Death to America” and “I swear by the blood of the supreme leader, Trump, we will kill you.”

Before arriving in Mashhad, Khamenei’s body was taken to the Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala for funeral processions.

According to the Iraqi paramilitary group Hashd al-Shaabi, more than 2.3 million people attended the procession in Najaf.

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and top commanders of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) also travelled to Iraq to participate in the procession.

Meanwhile, Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Ali Khamenei and Iran's newly incumbent supreme leader, has not participated in any of the public processions.

According to a New York Times report, his request to attend his father’s funeral was denied amid fears that he could be assassinated.

He has remained entirely out of public view since taking power just days after his father’s assassination.

articles.title: The Bull Case For CoStar Group (CSGP) Could Change Following Its French CRE Data Platform Launch
articles.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) has launched its commercial real estate intelligence platform in France, providing a comprehensive dataset for the French market. This expansion strengthens CoStar's international growth catalyst, but investors should consider the tension between short-term...
articles.content: CoStar Group (CSGP) has launched its commercial real estate intelligence platform in France, providing a comprehensive dataset for the French market. This expansion strengthens CoStar's international growth catalyst, but investors should consider the tension between short-term sentiment, affected by index removal, and the long-term thesis that substantial data investments and global reach will support future earnings, despite potential margin pressure from heavy international build-out. Analyst forecasts project $5.0 billion revenue and $679.3 million earnings by 2029, with a fair value of $48.25 suggesting a 64% upside.

articles.title: What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance interest rates?
articles.summary: Knowing today's mortgage rates can help new homebuyers and homeowners looking to lock in a good deal.
articles.content:
If you're , or you're thinking about , you may have faced some challenges. With high mortgage rates and a limited inventory of homes to contend with, it can be . But, doing so isn't impossible. That's especially true if you have a and overall application.

Of course, if you're planning to or considering refinancing your home, it's important to know what mortgage rates you can expect to see. We've compiled a list of today's average mortgage and refinance interest rates below.

What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance rates?

The following rates are current as of July 9, 2026. While these are averages based on national data, the actual mortgage loan and mortgage refinancing rates that you can qualify for may vary depending on your location, credit, loan type and other factors.

Average mortgage rates today:

  • 15-year mortgage: 6.00%
  • 30-year mortgage: 6.63%

Average refinance rates today:

  • 15-year refinance: 5.98%
  • 30-year refinance: 6.89%

How to get a great mortgage or refinance rate today

Even though interest rates are higher than they were a few years ago, both new homebuyers and current homeowners looking to refinance can secure a good rate today.

"Over the past two years, as the Fed has increased interest rates, borrowing has become more expensive," says Taylor Jessee, CFP, founder of Impact Financial. "For example, in 2020 you could lock in a mortgage rate between 2-3% easily. Nowadays mortgage rates are closer to 6-7%. If the Fed stops raising rates then I'd anticipate mortgage rates to also stop going up. That's good news for borrowers."

While the macro rate environment may be moving in your favor, there are also things you can do to improve your chances of qualifying for a great rate.

Perhaps most importantly, start by strengthening your credit profile.

"The best rates go to borrowers with credit scores in the mid- to high-700s or above," the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's website states. "These borrowers typically also have the most choices available to them."

Pay your bills in full and on time each month, look for any errors on your and avoid applying for other loans or credit cards too close to your mortgage or refinance application.

You should also shop around and compare different lenders. If you're refinancing, specifically, don't hesitate to look beyond your current mortgage lender. You may qualify for different rates and terms, which can make a big difference in what you pay over the lifetime of the loan.

What to look for in a mortgage or refinance loan

One of the first things you may want to consider when choosing a mortgage loan or refinancing is your preferred loan term. Shorter terms (15-year loans) generally offer better interest rates than longer terms (30-year).

Fees are another detail to factor into your overall payment. These can vary depending on your loan type and the closing costs charged by your lender. Both your fees and interest rate can affect your monthly payment. Before you take on any new loan, calculate your monthly payment to make sure you can afford it.

If you're refinancing, make sure you compare all of the details of the new loan you're approved for against your existing mortgage. Even if you get a better rate, other factors (like closing costs) could ultimately end up costing you more in the long run.

And don't forget to lock in your mortgage rate after you've made your decision, so you can rest assured that it won't increase again before you actually close on your home.

The bottom line

While mortgage rates are higher than they were a few years ago, both new homebuyers and existing homeowners who want to refinance still have plenty of good options. However, it's still important to compare a few different lenders and see what the best rates and loan terms are that you can qualify for before locking in a rate. Also, make sure to calculate your overall cost and payments so you're prepared to start paying down your new loan.

Ready to take on a new mortgage? Explore the best rates you can get today here!

articles.title: Corebridge Financial (NYSE:CRBG) Price Target Raised to $36.00

articles.content:
Corebridge Financial (NYSE:CRBG - Get Free Report) had its price target raised by stock analysts at Mizuho from $35.00 to $36.00 in a research note issued to investors on Thursday,Benzinga reports. The firm presently has an "outperform" rating on the stock. Mizuho's price objective would indicate a potential upside of 20.76% from the company's current price.

Other research analysts have also recently issued research reports about the stock. Keefe, Bruyette & Woods raised their price target on shares of Corebridge Financial from $31.00 to $38.00 and gave the stock an "outperform" rating in a report on Wednesday, May 6th. Wells Fargo & Company boosted their price objective on shares of Corebridge Financial from $32.00 to $35.00 and gave the company an "overweight" rating in a report on Tuesday, May 12th. Piper Sandler cut their target price on Corebridge Financial from $35.00 to $31.00 and set an "overweight" rating on the stock in a research report on Tuesday, May 26th. JPMorgan Chase & Co. cut their price objective on Corebridge Financial from $37.00 to $36.00 and set a "neutral" rating on the stock in a report on Tuesday, May 12th. Finally, Barclays lifted their target price on Corebridge Financial from $30.00 to $33.00 and gave the stock an "overweight" rating in a report on Tuesday. Eight research analysts have rated the stock with a Buy rating and six have assigned a Hold rating to the stock. According to MarketBeat.com, the company has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" and an average price target of $35.00.

Get Our Latest Stock Report on CRBG

Corebridge Financial Stock Down 2.6%

Corebridge Financial stock opened at $29.81 on Thursday. The company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.99, a current ratio of 0.12 and a quick ratio of 0.12. The stock has a market capitalization of $13.29 billion, a price-to-earnings ratio of 54.20, a PEG ratio of 0.34 and a beta of 1.10. Corebridge Financial has a 1-year low of $22.19 and a 1-year high of $36.57. The business has a fifty day moving average price of $27.95 and a 200-day moving average price of $27.86.

Corebridge Financial (NYSE:CRBG - Get Free Report) last announced its earnings results on Wednesday, May 6th. The company reported $1.05 earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter, missing the consensus estimate of $1.07 by ($0.02). The business had revenue of $4.08 billion for the quarter, compared to analyst estimates of $5.06 billion. Corebridge Financial had a net margin of 1.20% and a return on equity of 18.44%. During the same period in the prior year, the company earned $1.16 earnings per share. Equities research analysts forecast that Corebridge Financial will post 4.75 EPS for the current fiscal year.

Insider Activity at Corebridge Financial

In related news, insider David Ditillo sold 4,250 shares of the firm's stock in a transaction dated Monday, July 6th. The stock was sold at an average price of $30.00, for a total transaction of $127,500.00. Following the sale, the insider directly owned 128,153 shares of the company's stock, valued at $3,844,590. This trade represents a 3.21% decrease in their ownership of the stock. The transaction was disclosed in a legal filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available at this hyperlink. The transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. 0.29% of the stock is owned by insiders.

Institutional Trading of Corebridge Financial

Several hedge funds and other institutional investors have recently added to or reduced their stakes in the company. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. grew its holdings in Corebridge Financial by 229.9% in the first quarter. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. now owns 1,300,030 shares of the company's stock valued at $41,042,000 after purchasing an additional 905,918 shares during the period. Focus Partners Wealth purchased a new stake in shares of Corebridge Financial during the first quarter worth about $384,000. Creative Planning acquired a new position in shares of Corebridge Financial during the 2nd quarter worth about $243,000. EverSource Wealth Advisors LLC raised its position in shares of Corebridge Financial by 58.8% during the 2nd quarter. EverSource Wealth Advisors LLC now owns 4,558 shares of the company's stock worth $162,000 after purchasing an additional 1,688 shares during the last quarter. Finally, Cerity Partners LLC lifted its stake in Corebridge Financial by 64.4% in the 2nd quarter. Cerity Partners LLC now owns 92,554 shares of the company's stock valued at $3,286,000 after buying an additional 36,239 shares in the last quarter. Institutional investors and hedge funds own 98.25% of the company's stock.

Corebridge Financial Company Profile

(

Get Free Report

Corebridge Financial NYSE: CRBG is a publicly traded provider of retirement, life insurance and asset management solutions. Formed from the separation of American International Group’s life and retirement operations, Corebridge focuses on helping individuals, employers and institutions manage retirement income, protect against longevity and mortality risks, and invest long-term savings. The company operates under a unified brand that brings together insurance products and investment capabilities to deliver integrated financial solutions.

Corebridge’s product suite includes retirement income and annuity products, individual and group life insurance, asset management and investment advisory services, and employer-sponsored retirement plan offerings.

articles.title: VICI Properties keeps its focus on casino real estate. The REIT stays tied to long-term leases.
articles.summary: VICI Properties is a real estate investment trust focused on owning gaming and hospitality properties, leasing them out on long-term, triple-net lease agreements. Its business model centers on collecting recurring lease income, making the stability of its tenant base and portf...
articles.content:
VICI Properties (ISIN US9256521090) is a New York-listed real estate investment trust that owns gaming and hospitality properties and leases them on long-term, contract-based terms. The company trades on the NYSE under VICI, giving U.S. investors a direct way to follow the landlord side of the casino business.

Lease income drives the model

VICI's business model is built around owning large-scale destination properties and collecting rent rather than operating the casinos itself. That structure has made recurring lease income the key driver for the company, with the balance sheet and portfolio mix carrying more weight than short-term operating swings.

The setup also ties VICI closely to the health of major gaming operators and the durability of their properties. For investors, the question is less about a single quarter and more about how stable the tenant base remains over time.

U.S. market context

As a U.S.-listed REIT, VICI sits within the broader universe of income-oriented stocks watched by American retail investors and yield-focused funds. That positioning gives the company a clear market identity: property ownership, contracted cash flow, and exposure to the gaming real estate segment.

Recent trading attention across REITs has centered on rent coverage, capital allocation, and funding costs. Those are the same variables that matter for VICI because they shape how much room the company has to grow its asset base and sustain distributions.

Portfolio and tenants

The company's portfolio is anchored by gaming-related real estate, and that concentration defines its risk and return profile. The landlord model is straightforward: lease assets, collect rent, and use scale to support growth.

A first.

VICI stock and venue

VICI Properties stock trades on the NYSE in U.S. dollars. The shares were last priced at $0.00 as of July 9, 2026, 11:22 a.m. ET.

About United Rentals

United Rentals, Inc. is the largest equipment rental company in the world. The company has an integrated network of 1,658 rental locations in North America, 44 in Europe, 46 in Australia and 19 in New Zealand. In North America, the company operates in 49 states and every Canadian province. The company’s approximately 27,900 employees serve construction and industrial customers, utilities, municipalities, homeowners and others. The company offers a fleet of equipment for rent with a total original cost of $22.59 billion. United Rentals is a member of the Standard & Poor’s 500 Index, the Barron’s 400 Index and the Russell 3000 Index® and is headquartered in Stamford, Conn. Additional information about United Rentals is available at .

articles.title: Ask HN: What's the Fuzz about Taste?
articles.summary: For different reasons in the last 2 weeks I've found myself speaking to CTOs / CPOs that believe "Taste" is the main distinction and value a human brings to the process in a LLM/Generative AI world. I'm simply not getting it, and I don't know what I'm missing. + Self-driving a...
articles.content:
For different reasons in the last 2 weeks I've found myself speaking to CTOs / CPOs that believe "Taste" is the main distinction and value a human brings to the process in a LLM/Generative AI world.

I'm simply not getting it, and I don't know what I'm missing.

+ Self-driving as an example: We only really care if it drives better or equal to a human. So no taste applies there, once the standards are met.

Don't get me wrong, I understand (and share) that we use a concept of "taste" to actually mean "there is no enough data for the model to give a right answer", and the "judgement we introduce after years of living may be better". But I don't buy into the idea that exercising that decision (our free will if we may), is the actual difference simple because it is perceived as less stochastic.

So, is taste another way of saying you still know better? Or am I truly, and likely, missing something here?

PS: Apologies for overly simplifying the concept.

articles.title: Chipotle Stock Has a $43 Analyst Target. The Bull Case Could Go Higher.
articles.summary: Despite a recent drop in stock price, major analysts maintain a bullish outlook on Chipotle (CMG) with a mean target of $43, suggesting a 29% upside from its current $33 price. The company posted positive transaction growth in Q1 FY26 due to menu innovations and new equipment,...
articles.content:
Key Takeaways for Chipotle Mexican Grill Stock as of July 2026

  • 25 of 36 covering analysts rate Chipotle stock a buy or outperform, with the mean target at $43 leaving a 29% gap to the current $33 price.
  • TIKR’s mid-case model targets $65 by December 2030, a 94% total return annualizing to 16%.
  • At $33, Chipotle stock looks undervalued against an EBITDA growth curve that swings from -5% year-over-year to +19% by March 2027 as pricing catches up to inflation and the equipment rollout scales.
  • On June 5, J.P. Morgan upgraded to overweight after shares dropped 24% year-to-date.

Chipotle Stock Posted Positive Transaction Growth in Q1 While Investing Margins Into Long-Term Demand

Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) reported Q1 FY26 revenue of $3.1 billion, up 7.4% year-over-year, with comparable restaurant sales rising 0.5% on the first positive transaction growth the company has delivered since mid-2024.

That transaction turn came despite a January winter storm that shuttered roughly half of Chipotle’s 4,100 restaurants for several days, costing an estimated 100 basis points of comp.

What drove the recovery was a sharper innovation cadence: the high-protein menu campaign, the return of Chicken al Pastor, and the launch of Cilantro Lime Sauce, which outperformed every prior sauce offering at 2x the incidence rate of Red Chimichurri.

Behind the menu hits, the high-efficiency equipment package (dual-sided plancha, 3-pan rice cooker, high-capacity fryer) reached 600 restaurants in the quarter, up from 350 the prior period. CEO Scott Boatwright addressed the equipment’s impact directly on the Q1 earnings call: “What we’re seeing right now is outperformance on throughput, taste of food, OSAT, and then again hundreds of basis points of comp lift in those restaurants.” Management targets 2,000 installations by year-end, with the full system complete by late 2027 or early 2028.

Still, Chipotle stock carries a margin overhang. Adjusted restaurant-level margin fell 250 basis points year-over-year to 23.7% as the company ran just 0.9% price against roughly 4% industry inflation. CFO Adam Rymer framed the gap as deliberate: an investment in the value proposition while consumer sentiment stays weak.

That pricing discipline preserves long-term demand but compresses near-term EBITDA, which fell 12% year-over-year to $510 million. The question for Chipotle stock is how fast the transaction tailwinds from equipment, LTOs, and catering compound into earnings power as pricing gradually steps up through the back half.

25 of 36 Analysts Rate Chipotle Stock a Buy, With a $43 Mean Target

Of the 36 analysts covering Chipotle stock, 25 rate it a buy or outperform, 10 hold, one has no opinion, and zero recommend selling. The consensus mean target of $43 sits 29% above the current $33 price, with estimates ranging from $35 at the low end to $52 at the high.

J.P. Morgan’s June 5 upgrade to overweight reinforced the buy-side lean, though Morgan Stanley moved the opposite direction on June 3, cutting to equal-weight with a $37 target on slower margin recovery concerns.

Wall Street Expects Chipotle Stock’s EBITDA to Reaccelerate 19% by March 2027

Chipotle posted Q1 EBITDA of $510 million on a 16.6% margin, down from $670 million and 21.8% a year earlier.

Consensus expects the contraction to bottom quickly. The Street projects EBITDA of $630 million for the June 2026 quarter, still 5% below the prior year, but the year-over-year trajectory flips positive in September at +2% and accelerates through December (+10%) and March 2027 (+19%).

EBITDA margins follow the same arc. After bottoming at 17% in December 2025, margins recovered to 19% by March 2026, and consensus projects them reaching 20% by June 2027 as pricing steps closer to inflation and transaction growth compounds.

Whether that curve holds depends on how fast Chipotle closes its pricing gap. Management guided 1% to 2% for the full year against roughly 4% cost-of-sales inflation, and if the step-ups stall, EBITDA recovery flattens even as transactions improve.

TIKR’s $65 Target on Chipotle Stock Holds If EBITDA Reacceleration Follows the Transaction Turn

TIKR’s mid-case model values Chipotle stock at $65 by December 2030, implying 94% total return from the current price of $33, or 16% annualized over 4.5 years.

That 16% annualized return prices Chipotle well above the broader market but below the company’s own 10-year total return of 315%.

The path to $65 runs through the EBITDA recovery the Street already models: pricing that narrows the gap to inflation while transaction growth from the equipment rollout and LTO cadence compounds. With 2,000 stores on the new equipment by December and catering scaling toward a national launch in 2027, the building blocks sit inside management’s control rather than depending on a macro rebound.

Should You Invest in Chipotle Mexican Grill?

The only way to really know is to look at the numbers yourself. TIKR gives you free access to the same institutional-quality financial data that professional analysts use to answer exactly that question.

Pull up Chipotle Mexican Grill stock and you’ll see years of historical financials, what Wall Street analysts expect for revenue and earnings in the quarters ahead, how valuation multiples have moved over time, and whether price targets are trending up or down.

You can build a free watchlist to track Chipotle Mexican Grill alongside every other stock on your radar. No credit card required. Just the data you need to decide for yourself.

Looking for New Opportunities?

  • See what stocks billionaire investors are buying so you can follow the smart money.
  • Analyze stocks in as little as 5 minutes with TIKR’s all-in-one, easy-to-use platform.
  • The more rocks you overturn… the more opportunities you’ll uncover. Search 100K+ global stocks, global top investor holdings, and more with TIKR.

TIKR has no position in any stocks mentioned. Thank you for reading, and happy investing!

articles.title: Burlington Stores stock holds steady on off-price demand
articles.summary: Burlington Stores (BURL) stock remains driven by strong demand in the off-price retail segment, as the company focuses on expanding its national store base and leveraging its value-first business model. The off-price model, which relies on discounted branded merchandise and qu...
articles.content:
Burlington Stores (US12169T1007) stock is anchored by the off-price model that has made the retailer one of the more closely watched names in US value retail. The company operates Burlington stores across the United States and sells branded merchandise at discounted prices, a format that remains central to its investment case.

Business model first

For investors, the key question is not fashion trends alone but whether Burlington can keep attracting budget-conscious shoppers while protecting margins. Off-price retail is built on buying opportunities and fast inventory turnover, so execution at the merchandise level matters more than a standard full-price chain.

Burlington's footprint gives it scale, but scale also raises the stakes for rent, freight and labor control. That combination makes the model sensitive to any shift in consumer spending patterns, especially when households remain selective about discretionary purchases.

What matters now

A useful way to read Burlington is against the wider US off-price group, where value positioning often draws traffic before it converts into sales growth. That makes the company a direct test of whether the discount channel can keep winning share without relying on aggressive markdowns.

The stock's appeal is tied to that operating leverage. If buying conditions stay favorable and store productivity improves, Burlington can turn a basic retail formula into stronger earnings quality.

Store model

Burlington Stores Inc. focuses on apparel, footwear, home goods and accessories for value-oriented shoppers. The company's product mix is broad by design, because breadth helps off-price chains capture demand across categories rather than depending on one seasonal winner.

Trading context

Burlington Stores stock trades on the New York Stock Exchange in US dollars, and the shares remain a pure read on US discretionary spending and discount retail behavior. As of July 9, 2026, 1:06 p.m. UTC, the stock price was not included here.

This article was generated automatically and technically checked before publication. Price and company data without guarantee; prices and dates may change at short notice. Not investment advice, not a buy or sell recommendation. Trading in securities carries risks up to total loss.

articles.title: Grupo Cibest vende Banitsmo a Grupo BSC por USD 1.418 millones
articles.summary: Grupo Cibest S.A., parent company of Bancolombia, has sold its entire stake in the Panamanian bank Banistmo to Grupo Financiero BSC for USD 1.418 million. The acquisition, which closed on June 30 after regulatory approvals, was partially funded by a USD 350 million syndicated...
articles.content:
Grupo Cibest S.A., matriz de Bancolombia, vendió la totalidad de las acciones del banco panameño Banistmo por 1.418 millones de dólares. La negociación incluye a las sociedades subordinadas de la empresa objetivo, entre ellas Banistmo Investment Corporation, S.A. y Leasing Banistmo, S.A.

El comprador es Grupo Financiero BSC (anteriormente Inversiones Cuscatlán Centroamérica S.A.) con presencia regional y participación en negocios bancarios y aseguradores en El Salvador, Panamá, Guatemala, Honduras y Colombia.

El grupo realizó la operación a través de su filial Banco La Hipotecaria. Al mismo tiempo, obtuvo un préstamo a plazo sénior garantizado y un préstamo puente sénior garantizado de un sindicato de bancos, liderado por Banco Latinoamericano de Comercio Exterior (Bladex) y Banco Santander, por USD 350 millones. Con los recursos, el grupo financiará parcialmente la adquisición de Banitsmo.

El contrato de promesa de compraventa fue celebrado en diciembre pasado. La transacción cerró el 30 de junio tras obtener las aprobaciones regulatorias correspondientes.

En la compraventa, Morgan & Morgan (Ciudad de Panamá) y Milbank - Estados Unidos (Nueva York y Washington, D.C.) asesoraron a la compradora y Sullivan & Cromwell - Estados Unidos (Nueva York) y Alemán, Cordero, Galindo & Lee (Ciudad de Panamá) asistieron al vendedor.

En el financiamiento, Arias - El Salvador (San Salvador) representó a los prestamistas en el que es considerado uno de los financiamientos más importantes del sector bancario en Centroamérica.

Reconocido como uno de los principales bancos comerciales privados de Panamá por volumen de depósitos y créditos, Banistmo ofrece una gama de productos servicios financieros, que incluye cuentas, depósitos, tarjetas de crédito, préstamos, transferencias internacionales y seguros y asistencias, entre otros. Atiende una cartera de más de más de 600.000 clientes, entre personas, pymes y empresas.

Con esta nueva adquisición, Grupo BSC fortalece su presencia en Panamá, donde compró La Hipotecaria (Holding), Inc., sociedad holding de Banco La Hipotecaria, el año pasado, operación que también le ayudó a expandirse en Colombia y El Salvador.

Asesores legales

Asesores de Grupo Financiero BSC:

  • Morgan & Morgan (Ciudad de Panamá): Socios Roberto Vidal, Ana Carolina Castillo, José Rafael Reyes y Sophia Lee. Asociados sénior Miguel Arias Mamais y Allen Candanedo. Asociados David Ramos, Ariana Linares, Arantxa Fernández y Eduardo Oteiza.

Asesores de Inversiones Cuscatlán Centroamérica S.A.:

  • Milbank - Estados Unidos (Nueva York y Washington, D.C.): Socios Francisco Núñez, Marcelo Mottesi, Scott Golenbock, Max Goodman y Bijan Ganji. Consejeros especiales Gonzalo Guitart, John Garces y Pinky Mehta. Abogado internacional Fernando Vial Comber. Asociados Gabe Carolan, Marco Zaldivar y Jesse Hong.

Asesores de Cibest Group S.A.:

  • Sullivan & Cromwell - Estados Unidos (Nueva York): Socios Sergio J. Galvis y Stephen M. Salley. Asociado Ethan J. Chess.
  • Alemán, Cordero, Galindo & Lee (Ciudad de Panamá): Socios Arturo Gerbaud, Rafael Marquínez y Patricia Cordero.

° Operación 2 (Financiamiento)

Asesores de Banco Latinoamericano de Comercio Exterior (Bladex), Banco Santander y demás prestamistas:

  • Arias - El Salvador (San Salvador): Socia Ana Mercedes López. Consejero sénior Rafael Burgos.

articles.title: Show HN: Arcaide – Explore code with multi-level call graphs
articles.summary: One of the things I do when approaching a new codebase is to find the entry points and start exploring down the call paths. This gives a good overview of the different components in the code and how they're connected. I wanted to translate that to a visual experience, similar...
articles.content:
One of the things I do when approaching a new codebase is to find the entry points and start exploring down the call paths. This gives a good overview of the different components in the code and how they're connected. I wanted to translate that to a visual experience, similar to how you would use call graphs, but there's a couple of problems with classical call graphs. One, call graphs represent flow at the function level, so the architectural context is lost. And call graphs tend to get very large and can grow exponentially with program size.

The approach I'm exploring is to construct "multi-level" call graphs. These are call graphs represented in the form of program structure showing control flow not just at the function level, but rolled up to higher level units such as classes and packages. This gives you the ability to zoom in and out and see your code at different levels of abstraction, à la C4 diagrams, allowing you to navigate large graphs by expanding the areas you care about and collapsing the rest.

The graph is then fed to an LLM for semantic analysis. This does two things, detects telemetry, trivial utilities to strip them out of the graph further condensing it, and identifies external interfaces and dependencies to enrich the graph. The result is a single graph which incorporates both structural and behavioral aspects. You can see package level dependencies, class composition and relationships, as well as external services, databases and user interactions. Think of it as a package diagram + class diagram + use case diagram combined into a single composite diagram.

Of course, ultimately source is king. There is no substitute for reading code to understand the details of what it is doing. But a map doesn't replace the terrain, it tells you where to walk. As we shift from hand coding each line to orchestrating agents that are generating all the code, maintaining the "big picture" becomes ever more important. We need better maps to help us navigate the terrain.

I would love to hear what you think though. Do check out some of the example diagrams in the link [1] and share your feedback. Also interested in your general thoughts on program comprehension!

[1] Link: https://arcaide.foo

articles.title: Parent company of Hong Kong IVF clinic says sorry for embryo specimen mix-up
articles.summary: The parent group of a Hong Kong fertility clinic involved in a rare mix-up of embryo biopsy specimens has apologised for the incident, pledging to cooperate with the authorities’ investigation and providing support to affected patients. New Frontier Group, the parent company o...
articles.content:
Parent company of Hong Kong IVF clinic says sorry for embryo specimen mix-up

New Frontier Group vows to launch sweeping internal review into cause of mix-up at Heal Fertility’s clinic in Central

The parent group of a Hong Kong fertility clinic involved in a rare mix-up of embryo biopsy specimens has apologised for the incident, pledging to cooperate with the authorities’ investigation and provide support to affected patients.

The assisted reproductive clinic in Central was found to have mishandled embryo biopsy specimens from two patients in May and June, but failed to promptly report the incident to authorities.

The clinic has been ordered to submit a report within four weeks, explaining the cause of the incident and detailing improvement measures.

The Council on Human Reproductive Technology, the Department of Health and police have all launched investigations into the error.

“Regarding the embryo biopsy specimens mix-up incident, the group expresses its sincere apology,” New Frontier said.

“The group attaches great importance to the incident and has established an internal investigation team to thoroughly examine the causes and the management systems and procedures.”
